history.text: Uzbek khanates ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
Bukharan People's Soviet Republic introduced a new form of state rule.
Uzbek SSR placed the territory inside the Soviet administrative system.
Uzbekistan became independent from the Soviet Union, marking a key step in modern statehood.
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Uzbekistan.

worthSeeing.title: Samarkand:
worthSeeing.text: Tamerlane's jewel on the Silk Road, with the Registan square of brilliant tiled madrasas.

worthSeeing.title: Bukhara:
worthSeeing.text: Ancient holy city of 140 mosques and the Ark fortress, frozen in Silk Road splendour.

worthSeeing.title: Khiva:
worthSeeing.text: Walled inner city of Itchan Kala, a UNESCO living museum of Islamic architecture.
